actionability.label: actionable
actionability.rationale: The component QueryPerformanceGrid has too many props, which is a recognized code smell. The props defined in the QueryPerformanceGridProps interface include aggregatedData, isLoading, error, currentSelectedQuery, onCurrentSelectQuery, onRetry, and onScroll. This indicates that the component is likely handling too many responsibilities, which can be simplified by splitting the component or extracting some of the props into a context or custom hook. The risk of breaking behavior is low to medium as the refactoring can be done in a controlled manner, focusing on reducing the number of props passed to the component.

invariants: Exported component name and its props API are unchanged
Sub-components are not exported (internal implementation detail)
plan.expected_smell_resolution: By splitting the QueryPerformanceGrid component into two focused sub-components, we can reduce the number of props passed to each sub-component, thereby addressing the 'Too Many Props' code smell. Each sub-component will handle a distinct responsibility, making the code cleaner and easier to maintain.
abort_reasons: Responsibilities share too much state to separate cleanly
Split would result in more than 5 props being drilled through the coordinator
Component is already at or below 80 lines

issues: The plan did not result in any file changes, indicating that the refactor was not executed.
critique_result.feedback: Ensure that the refactor plan includes specific implementation steps to split the QueryPerformanceGrid component into two sub-components. Clearly outline how the props will be divided and how the internal state will be managed to avoid the abort reasons.
